title: KKR-Backed Ambulance Firm GMR Shrinks IPO Target to $479 Million
summary: GMR Solutions Inc., an air and ground emergency medical services company that counts KKR & Co. among its backers, revised the terms of its IPO and is seeking to raise $479 million, down from an earlier target of as much as $798 million.

title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Ex-Deutsche Bank Wealth Manager Leaves US Firm
summary: One of Jeffrey Epstein’s former top Deutsche Bank AG relationship managers left a financial services firm with ties to ultra-wealthy individuals caught up in US government files on the late financier.

title: Europe Struggling to Draw up Critical Minerals Battle Plan
summary: The continent has at times been slow to support the extraction and refinement of certain materials that are crucial for the defense industry. China and the US, meanwhile, are sprinting ahead.

title: DeepMind Spinout Isomorphic Labs Raises $2.1 Billion to Design Drugs With AI
summary: Alphabet Inc.’s Isomorphic Labs raised $2.1 billion in its second round of outside capital, with new support from sovereign wealth funds, as it seeks to make artificial intelligence-designed drugs a reality.

title: Macron Seeks New African Friends Across Language Barrier
summary: East Africa represents one of the few remaining growth frontiers where France believes it can expand its footprint.

title: Talking Transports: Morgan Stanley on Transport Disruption Risks
summary: Amazon.com’s expanding logistics ambitions are poised to disrupt multiple transportation segments, from parcel to brokerage and LTL. In this Talking Transports podcast, Morgan Stanley Managing Director Ravi Shanker joins Bloomberg Intelligence’s senior transportation and logistics analyst Lee Klaskow to discuss how Amazon’s scale and asset-light approach could pressure incumbents across freight markets. Shanker outlines structural challenges in parcel, including the unsustainability of free ship

title: Tech Disruptors: Atlassian CEO on Human-AI Agent Collaboration
summary: AI agents are reshaping enterprise workflows, increasing the importance of organizational context and connected data. Atlassian CEO and co-founder Mike Cannon-Brookes joins Bloomberg Intelligence senior software analyst Sunil Rajgopal to discuss how Atlassian is embedding AI across Jira, Confluence and service-management tools through its Rovo platform and Teamwork Graph. “The future is about human and agent collaboration,” Cannon-Brookes says. The discussion also covers enterprise AI adoption,

title: Inside Active: State Street’s Paglia on Performance and Scale
summary: Over the past decade, expanded market access through ETFs and new structures has raised questions about capacity, outcomes and what it means for active management. In this episode of the Inside Active podcast, host David Cohne, mutual fund and active management analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, speaks with Anna Paglia, executive vice president and chief business officer for State Street Investment Management, about how the industry is moving beyond the traditional active-versus-passive debate a

title: Tech Firm CGI Picks Tim Hurlebaus as CEO After AI Fears Crush Share Price
summary: Shares of Canadian IT consulting firm CGI Inc. extended their losses over 40% in one year after it appointed a new chief executive officer, a slump largely due to concerns about disruption from artificial intelligence upstarts such as Anthropic PBC.

title: Gold Slides Lower as US Inflation Clouds Fed Easing Outlook
summary: Gold fell as the latest US economic data showed inflation accelerated on rising energy and food prices, reducing odds of monetary easing by the Federal Reserve this year.

title: Chip Stocks Down on Momentum and Not CPI, Bokeh's Forrest Says
summary: Kim Forrest, Bokeh Capital Partners chief investment officer, says Tuesday's selloff is just a pause in momentum for skyrocketing chip stocks, not a shock from a hotter-than-estimated inflation report. She speaks with Ed Ludlow and Caroline Hyde on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US to Unveil New Data Spotlighting Hormuz and Global Reserves
summary: The US government’s energy statistics agency will start releasing new data on the world’s strategic reserves and flows of petroleum and liquefied natural gas through shipping choke points.

title: Chip Stocks Sink as Inflation Woes Boost US Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in high-flying chipmakers drove stocks lower, with the market also falling alongside bonds after inflation data showed the impacts of energy disruptions stemming from the war in Iran.

title: Chile’s Populist Renegade Is Getting His Way on Kast’s Big Bill
summary: Chile’s Franco Parisi, the third-place finisher in last year’s presidential election, is proving to be a formidable political operative, strong-arming the government to accept his populist initiatives in exchange for supporting its economic agenda.

title: Inflation to Peak in May or June, Gapen Says
summary: Morgan Stanley Chief US Economist expects US inflation to peak this year in May or June. He says the latest CPI report will keep the Federal Reserve on the sidelines for the rest of this year. He speaks on Bloomberg Surveillance.

title: Kazakh CPC Oil Exports to Drop as Europe Faces Tight Supplies
summary: Kazakhstan will cut crude exports from a key Russian Black Sea port next month at a time when European refineries are already grappling with an unprecedented supply disruption from the Middle East.

title: Biggest US Grid May Be Too Large to Function, Regulator Says
summary: PJM Interconnection LLC, the biggest power grid in the US, may have become too big to adequately function and needs urgent reform, Federal Energy Regulatory Commission Chair Laura Swett said at the grid operator’s annual meeting.

title: Alphabet Offers Eight-Tranche Deal in Its Debut Yen Bond Sale
summary: Alphabet Inc. has begun marketing its first-ever bond sale in yen, further broadening its funding channels as the Google parent sharply increases capital spending to finance artificial intelligence efforts.

title: JPMorgan Fully Committed to EU,  EMEA Co-Head Wiltz Says
summary: Matthieu Wiltz, JPMorgan EMEA co-CEO, says M&A, equity and debt capital markets, and leveraged finance had a “booming” first quarter with a solid pipeline, and that investor appetite and cash levels support decent performance in coming months despite geopolitical uncertainty. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Soy Growers Want Commitments From China as Season Slips Away
summary: As US President Donald Trump travels to Beijing for a summit this week with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, American soybean farmers are in a now-familiar spot: Fields are getting planted when demand from their biggest customer is uncertain.

title: Ex-Eaton Vance CEO Takes Majority Stake in Mutual Fund Rescuer
summary: Thomas Faust, who engineered the sale of Eaton Vance to Morgan Stanley, is beginning his next act: helping out mutual fund managers who are facing redemption requests.

title: Treasuries Fall as Rising Oil Prices Herald Sticky Inflation
summary: Treasuries fell as rising oil prices threatened to keep inflation at levels that could prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates next year.

title: Copper Rallies Above $14,000 a Ton, Nearing Fresh All-Time High
summary: Copper jumped above $14,000 a ton, closing in on a record high as a rebound in Chinese demand and mounting supply risks outweigh concerns about the Iran war’s impact on global growth.

title: Dimon Says JPMorgan Would Scrap New UK HQ Over Higher Bank Taxes
summary: Jamie Dimon warned the UK that any move to hike taxes on banks in the event Keir Starmer is replaced as the UK’s prime minster would see JPMorgan Chase & Co. scrap plans to invest billions in a new London headquarters in Canary Wharf.

title: Oil Extends Gain as Trump Casts Doubt Over Ceasefire With Iran
summary: Oil pushed higher as US President Donald Trump cast doubt over the ceasefire with Iran after rejecting Tehran’s latest peace offer, prolonging the effective closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Household Debt Delinquencies Stay Flat in First Quarter
summary: The share of overdue consumer loans stayed flat in the first quarter of 2026 as new delinquencies edged down.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 5/12/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Energy Supply to Dominate Indian Prime Minister Modi’s UAE Visit
summary: Energy will dominate Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to the United Arab Emirates on Friday as India moves to shore up stable supplies amid rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

title: Supply-Chain Stress That Peaked in Covid Heads Higher Again
summary: The impact of the energy crisis on the global economy in recent months is showing up in gauges of supply-chain stress that flashed red during the pandemic, adding to reasons for central banks to be on guard for a recurrence of high inflation.

title: Vietnam Urges US Navy to Let Oil Tanker Through Its Blockade
summary: Vietnam’s state oil company urged the US to let a supertanker pass through an American naval blockade outside of the Persian Gulf, saying the shipment is critical to the Asian country’s economy.

title: DoorDash Poses Disruption Risk to Payments Firms, Analyst Says
summary: An expected point-of-sale technology rollout from DoorDash Inc. is poised to deal a blow to payments firms such as Toast Inc., according to Rothschild & Co Redburn’s Dominic Ball.

title: Under Armour Slides on Guidance Miss; Wendy's Up on Private Operator Bid | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Under Armour (UAA) shares are sliding after it forecast adjusted earnings per share for 2027 and the guidance missed the average analyst estimate.
- Wendy's (WEN) shares are gaining as Financial Times reports that Nelson Peltz’s Trian Fund Management is seeking investor backing for a bid to take the burger chain operator private.
- Hims & Hers (HIMS) shares are lower after reporting a first-quarter loss and sales that missed Wall Street estimates due to higher costs associated with its strategic pivot to branded weight-loss medications. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Inflation Accelerates as Gas, Food Prices Climb
summary:
US inflation accelerated in April on rising gasoline and grocery costs, exceeding wage growth in a double-whammy for already strained consumers.
Eric Winograd, Chief US Economist at AllianceBernstein, reacts to the CPI data. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Mania Makes Old-School Industrials Behave Like Chip Stocks
summary: Optimism surrounding the potential for industrial companies to profit from the artificial intelligence boom has fueled record-setting momentum in the sector. Now worries are mounting that the group’s link to AI may be getting too tight.

title: Amazon Sells Swiss Franc Bond Offering in Record Six Parts
summary: Amazon.com Inc. sold its first Swiss franc bonds across a record six tranches, as Big Tech looks beyond its regular debt markets to raise funds.

title: Dimon Says Effects of Iran War Are Getting More Serious Each Day
summary: JPMorgan Chase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon says the effects of the Iran war are getting more serious each day. He also says the wealthier American consumer "is spending like they should." He speaks to Bloomberg's Francine Lacqua at the bank’s annual Global Markets Conference in Paris.  (Source: Bloomberg)
